title: Winey Crispy Thai Morbier Tagine
description: A quick and aromatic weeknight dish that marries the sharpness of Morbier cheese with the bright flavors of Thai cuisine, finished in a winey, crispy tagine.
instructions: Marinate the cheese slices in white wine for 15 minutes.
Heat olive oil in a tagine or heavy skillet over medium-high until shimmering.
Place the cheese slices in the hot oil and sear until golden and crispy on both sides, then remove and set aside.
Add ginger, lemongrass, and kaffir lime leaves to the same pan and sauté for 1 minute.
Pour in coconut milk, coconut aminos, and lime juice, and bring to a gentle simmer.
Return the cheese slices to the pan and simmer for 3 minutes, allowing the flavors to meld.
Stir in fresh coriander leaves and remove the pan from heat.
Serve the dish hot, garnished with extra coriander and a wedge of lime if desired.
